req.title: Lab order routing (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall order a lab test; orders shall route to the patient's preferred lab when available. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.
error_pathways: fallback to default lab when preferred unavailable

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-093 requires three distinct test paths: (1) happy-path routing to the patient's preferred lab with AuditEvent emitted (EP — valid partition where preferred lab is available), (2) fallback routing when the preferred lab is unavailable (NEG/DT — decision table row where preferred lab down), and (3) a boundary case exercising the state transition from 'draft' through 'active' to 'completed' on a ServiceRequest with audit trail at each state (STC). Each test is isolated via fixture-supplied auth tokens and patient IDs, hits /ServiceRequest and /AuditEvent endpoints, and asserts both business routing logic and audit compliance.

tests.name: test_order_routes_to_preferred_lab_with_audit
preconditions: Patient record exists with a preferred lab set to 'LabCorp-001'
LabCorp-001 is available and accepting orders
actions: POST /ServiceRequest with subject referencing the patient and requester referencing the clinician
expected: Response body contains performer reference matching 'LabCorp-001'
At least one AuditEvent is returned for the ServiceRequest
trace: R-HLT-093: order routed to preferred lab
R-HLT-093: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_order_routes_to_preferred_lab_with_audit(
    clinician_auth_headers,   # <fixture>
    preferred_patient_id,     # <fixture> patient with preferred lab LabCorp-001
    base_url,                 # <fixture>
    make_service_request_payload,  # <helper_fn>
    assert_audit_event,       # <helper_fn>
):
    payload = make_service_request_payload(
        patient_id=preferred_patient_id,
        code="58410-2",
    )
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/ServiceRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, resp.text
    body = resp.json()
    sr_id = body["id"]
    performers = [p.get("reference", "") for p in body.get("performer", [])]
    assert any("LabCorp-001" in p for p in performers), "Preferred lab not selected"
    assert body["status"] == "active"
    assert_audit_event(
        base_url=base_url,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
        entity_id=sr_id,
        expected_action="C",
        expected_outcome="0",
    )

tests.name: test_order_falls_back_to_default_lab_when_preferred_unavailable
preconditions: Patient record exists with preferred lab set to 'LabCorp-001'
LabCorp-001 is marked unavailable in the system (status=inactive)
A default lab 'DefaultLab-999' is configured and available
actions: POST /ServiceRequest with subject referencing the patient and requester referencing the clinician
expected: Response body performer reference matches 'DefaultLab-999' (fallback), not 'LabCorp-001'
At least one AuditEvent is returned
AuditEvent contains a note or extension indicating fallback routing
trace: R-HLT-093: fallback to default lab when preferred unavailable
R-HLT-093: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_order_falls_back_to_default_lab_when_preferred_unavailable(
    clinician_auth_headers,         # <fixture>
    unavailable_preferred_patient_id,  # <fixture> patient whose preferred lab is inactive
    base_url,                       # <fixture>
    make_service_request_payload,   # <helper_fn>
    assert_audit_event,             # <helper_fn>
    get_audit_events,               # <helper_fn>
):
    payload = make_service_request_payload(
        patient_id=unavailable_preferred_patient_id,
        code="58410-2",
    )
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/ServiceRequest",
        json=payload,
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, resp.text
    body = resp.json()
    sr_id = body["id"]
    performers = [p.get("reference", "") for p in body.get("performer", [])]
    assert not any("LabCorp-001" in p for p in performers), "Unavailable lab was selected"
    assert any("DefaultLab-999" in p for p in performers), "Fallback lab not selected"
    assert body["status"] == "active"
    events = get_audit_events(base_url, clinician_auth_headers, sr_id)
    assert len(events) >= 1
    notes = " ".join(str(e) for e in events)
    assert "fallback" in notes.lower() or "DefaultLab-999" in notes

tests.name: test_service_request_state_transition_draft_to_completed_with_audit_at_each_state
preconditions: Patient record exists with a preferred lab set to 'LabCorp-001'
actions: POST /ServiceRequest with status='draft' to create the order
PUT /ServiceRequest/<ID> with status='active' to activate the order
PUT /ServiceRequest/<ID> with status='completed' to complete the order
expected: First AuditEvent query returns exactly 1 event with action='C'
First PUT returns HTTP 200 with status='active'
Second AuditEvent query returns at least 2 events, latest action='U'
Second PUT returns HTTP 200 with status='completed'
Third AuditEvent query returns at least 3 events, latest action='U' for completed transition
trace: R-HLT-093: status tracked across order lifecycle
R-HLT-093: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
R-HLT-093: order routed to preferred lab
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_service_request_state_transition_draft_to_completed_with_audit_at_each_state(
    clinician_auth_headers,      # <fixture>
    preferred_patient_id,        # <fixture>
    base_url,                    # <fixture>
    make_service_request_payload,  # <helper_fn>
    get_audit_events,            # <helper_fn>
):
    payload = make_service_request_payload(
        patient_id=preferred_patient_id,
        code="58410-2",
        status="draft",
    )
    r1 = requests.post(f"{base_url}/ServiceRequest", json=payload, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert r1.status_code == 201
    sr = r1.json()
    sr_id, sr_ver = sr["id"], sr["meta"]["versionId"]
    assert sr["status"] == "draft"
    events = get_audit_events(base_url, clinician_auth_headers, sr_id)
    assert len(events) >= 1 and events[0]["action"] == "C"

    sr["status"] = "active"
    r2 = requests.put(f"{base_url}/ServiceRequest/{sr_id}", json=sr, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert r2.status_code == 200
    assert r2.json()["status"] == "active"
    events = get_audit_events(base_url, clinician_auth_headers, sr_id)
    assert len(events) >= 2
    assert events[-1]["action"] == "U"

    sr2 = r2.json()
    sr2["status"] = "completed"
    r3 = requests.put(f"{base_url}/ServiceRequest/{sr_id}", json=sr2, headers=clinician_auth_headers)
    assert r3.status_code == 200
    assert r3.json()["status"] == "completed"
    events = get_audit_events(base_url, clinician_auth_headers, sr_id)
    assert len(events) >= 3
    assert events[-1]["action"] == "U"
